教.origin: On the left is 孝 (showing care for elders); on the right 攵 is a hand with a stick, gently guiding learning.

sentence.en: The teacher teaches us to write Chinese characters.

汉.origin: The water drops 氵 on the left remind you of the great Han river that gave the Han people their name.

sentence.en: I can write many Chinese characters.

语.origin: The speech marks 讠 on the left mean words; add 吾 (me) and you get the language I speak.

歌.origin: Picture brother 哥 opening his mouth wide with a big breath 欠 to sing out a happy song.

画.origin: Imagine a framed field 田 inside a box — a finished picture hanging on the wall for everyone to see.

这.origin: The walking path 辶 brings something right up close to you — 这 means 'this,' the thing nearby.

又.origin: A simple drawing of a right hand reaching out — when you do something 'again,' your hand goes back for more.

桌.origin: Stand something tall 卓 on legs of wood 木 at the bottom, and you have a sturdy table.

sentence.en: The book is on the table.

椅.origin: Wood 木 on the left makes the seat; the part on the right tells you how to say it — a wooden chair.

两.origin: Look inside the little house and you can spot two people 从 standing side by side — that means 'two.'

边.origin: The walking path 辶 wraps around the edge of things — 边 means the side or border you walk along.

墙.origin: Earth 土 on the left reminds you that long ago people piled up soil to build a tall, strong wall.

sentence.en: There is a picture on the wall.

着.origin: An eye 目 keeps watching while something keeps happening — 着 shows an action carrying on right now.

sentence.en: He talks to me with a smile.

图.origin: A box 囗 draws a border all around, like the edge of a map — inside it you find a chart or picture.

角.origin: This character is shaped like an animal's pointy horn — and a horn is sharp at its corner or angle.

sentence.en: The cat sleeps in the corner.

星.origin: A sun 日 grows 生 small and bright in the night sky — that twinkling little sun is a star.

sentence.en: There are many stars in the sky.

期.origin: The moon 月 on the right marks time as it grows and shrinks, helping us count out a period of days.

sentence.en: I am very busy this week.

做.origin: A person 亻 on the left is busy making things happen — 做 means to do, work, or make something.

客.origin: Under a roof 宀 a visitor arrives 各 — a welcome guest stepping into your warm home.

sentence.en: Guests came to our home today.

本.origin: Draw a tree 木 and add one line 一 at the bottom to point out its roots — the very beginning of things.

玩.origin: The jade 王 on the left was a smooth, shiny stone people loved to hold and play with for fun.

形.origin: The three little strokes 彡 on the right are like light or lines showing the shape and form of a thing.

都.origin: The city part 阝 on the right gathers everyone in — 都 means 'all,' as if the whole town is together.

用.origin: Picture a bucket with handles, ready to be picked up and put to work — 用 means to use something.

记.origin: Speech marks 讠 on the left plus 己 (oneself) — words you note down for yourself so you remember.

请.origin: Speech marks 讠 on the left mean you are using polite words — 请 is how you kindly ask or invite.

谢.origin: Speech marks 讠 on the left mean kind words — 谢 is the warm word you say to thank someone.

对.origin: Two hands 又 and 寸 reaching toward each other and matching up — when things match, they are correct.

没.origin: Water 氵 on the left swallows things up until they sink away and are gone — 没 means 'not' or 'none.'

关.origin: Picture two doors shutting together tight — 关 means to close, like locking a frontier gate.

系.origin: The thread 糸 at the bottom is being tied up — 系 means to fasten or connect things into one system.

孩.origin: The child 子 on the left tells you at a glance that 孩 is all about a little baby or kid.

过.origin: The walking path 辶 on the left shows feet moving by — 过 means to pass by or to cross over.

sentence.en: Please come over for a moment.

昨.origin: The sun 日 on the left marks a day — 昨 is the day whose sun has already set: yesterday.

全.origin: Precious jade 玉 tucked safely 入 under a roof — every piece is there, so 全 means whole and complete.

会.origin: People 人 gathering together under one roof to talk 云 — when people meet, they can do things together.

给.origin: Thread 纟 on the left ties two people together as one hands a gift to the other — 给 means to give.

蛋.origin: The insect 虫 at the bottom reminds you that little creatures and birds lay eggs — 蛋 means egg.

sentence.en: I eat eggs in the morning.

送.origin: The walking path 辶 carries you along as you wave 关 to a friend — 送 means to see off or to give.

具.origin: Two hands 八 hold up a tablet 目 to write on — 具 means a tool or thing you use to get work done.

sentence.en: My pencil case is very pretty.
